title: R-TechApp Man Trap

The '''R-TechApp Man Trap''' was a [[reversed repulsorfield]] manufactured by [[Ubrikkian Transports/Legends|Ubrikkian Transports]].

==Description==
Advertised as a safe, non-lethal way to subdue a fleeing target,<ref name="FP">{{GamerCite|1|Fair Prey}}</ref> the R-TechApp Man Trap was a device developed by [[Ubrikkian Transports/Legends|Ubrikkian Transports]]. It was a one-[[Meter/Legends|meter]] square metal plate that stood barely five [[Centimeter/Legends|centimeters]] tall. The [[Power cell/Legends|power cells]] could provide charge for up to three [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] of operation.<ref name="EGWT" />

The R-TechApp had a remote activator that came with a 10-meter cord.<ref name="EGWT" /> Cords with varying lengths could be purchased and used to allow the operator to be hidden further away.<ref name="GG10" /> The device was rigged with [[gravfield generator]]s that could intensify the local [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] of a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] by a factor of eight. In normal operation, the gravfield generators were powered up. However, the operator could leave the generators inert and only activate them at the exact instant a target stepped over the device.<ref name="EGWT" />
When an individual walked over the trap, the extra gravities pulled down and immobilized by the increased weight of their own mass. Targets could then be [[Stun/Legends|stunned]], tranquilized, or interrogated. Calibrating the R-TechApp on a standard one-gravity planet, it required four to five extra gravities to restrain a [[Human/Legends|Human]]. However, this would not stop a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]]. A full setting of eight gravities would hold a Wookiee, but would crush a [[Mriss/Legends|Mrissi's]] bones.<ref name="EGWT" />

Setting up the R-TechApp Man Trap was extremely easy; it was placed flat on the ground then covered with topsoil, leaves, or other foliage to camouflage it. [[Infrared baffler]]s were used to mask the trap's heat emissions, specifically from [[Species/Legends|species]] capable of seeing in the [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]] spectrum.<ref name="EGWT" />

The R-TechApp model had a number of detrimental side effects, however. Although designed to be nonlethal, sprains, dislocations and broken [[Bone/Legends|bones]] were common as targets could fall at odd angles and have their increased mass cause an injury.<ref name="EGWT" /> A pinned subject's [[Heart/Legends|heart]] and [[Lung/Legends|lungs]] often struggled due to the heavy gravity, while vital fluids that were forced through grav-compressed passages could burst from the strain. [[Blood/Legends|Blood]] would pool in abdominal organs, which would cause the [[Brain/Legends|brain]] to starve from lack of [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]]. If the subject was not freed from the man trap, cardiac arrest was a likely outcome. [[Bounty hunter/Legends|Bounty hunters]] who didn't frequently check their man traps often found a [[Death/Legends|dead]] mark when they returned.<ref name="FP" />

==History==
The R-TechApp Man Trap was favored by [[Slaver/Legends|slavers]] and bounty hunters due to its ability to capture a target undamaged for live delivery. The units could also be integrated into security systems, when they could be used to stop intruders and effectively block entire corridors.<ref name="EGWT" />

The [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]] business magnate [[Tyro Viveca/Legends|Tyro Viveca]] had a R-TechApp hidden on the grounds of his estate, which he used during his hunting excursions. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the thief [[Cecil Noone]] was caught in one and barely managed to survive.<ref name="FP" />
